# legacy_gnome_visuals

Historical Python code for visualizing and analyzing the HyperGrid Transform — a neuromorphic encoding scheme that maps scalar/vector inputs into distributed binary representations across multiple offset grids.

**Static asset archive:** https://github.com/jacobeverist/legacy_gnome_gallery — rendered images and animations originally generated by this code, browseable via a gallery website.

## What This Is

A collection of standalone Python scripts organized by visualization approach. There is no unified application or library — each script is independently runnable and generates image or video output. The core encoding algorithm comes from the `brainblocks` library (`brainblocks.tools.HyperGridTransform`).

## Structure

| Directory | Description |
|---|---|
| `hypergrid_transform/` | Primary module — similarity heatmaps, animated grid visualizations, interval encoding illustrations, and encoding comparison experiments |
| `gnomes_to_graph/` | Graph and grid visualizations of hypergrid encoder spatial structure |
| `segmented_space_and_axes/` | Space segmentation visualizations |
| `encoder_analysis/` | Finite interval encoder and gnome similarity analysis |
| `discretized_sine_waves/` | 2D and 3D planar wave visualizations |
| `grid_helix/` | 3D helix and cylinder grid visualizations |
| `reference_code/` | Foundational utilities — core transform implementations, hex grid math, dataset generators |
| `seaborn_examples/` | Heatmap, cluster, and ridge plot demonstrations |
| `matplotlib_examples/` | Standalone matplotlib visualization examples |

## Setup

Each subdirectory has its own `requirements.txt`. Install dependencies for whichever module you want to run:

```bash
pip install -r hypergrid_transform/requirements.txt
```

Key dependencies: `matplotlib`, `seaborn`, `numpy`, `scipy`, `shapely`, `brainblocks`.

## Running Scripts

All scripts are self-contained. Run them from within their subdirectory so that relative output paths resolve correctly:

```bash
cd hypergrid_transform
python plot_hgt_visuals.py        # animated grid visualization (PNG frames)
python plot_similarity.py         # similarity heatmaps
python run_hypergrid_experiments.py  # encoding parameter sweep
python interval_graphs.py         # interval encoding illustrations
```

Output is written to an `out/` subdirectory within each module.

## Generating Videos and GIFs

Scripts that produce animations write a sequence of numbered PNG frames to `out/`. Convert frames to MKV and GIF using ffmpeg:

```bash
# MKV then GIF (palette-optimized)
ffmpeg -y -framerate 10 -i out/visual_test_frames_1_%05d.png -c:v libx264 -preset veryslow -crf 0 out/visual_test.mkv
ffmpeg -y -i out/visual_test.mkv -filter_complex "[0:v] split [a][b];[a] palettegen [p];[b][p] paletteuse" out/visual_test.gif
```

Each module's `encode_video.sh` contains the specific ffmpeg commands used to produce the archived assets.
